compiled_state.title: Automated Jira Bridge Configuration in Project Setup
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context
Teams onboarding to DSO's Jira bridge face an unnecessarily burdensome setup process. The project-setup wizard prompts users for values they shouldn't need to know — ACLI SHA256 checksums, specific ACLI version strings, and bridge bot identity details — when the installer can resolve all of these automatically. Step 6.5 currently presents a series of manual prompts asking the user to enter ACLI_VERSION, ACLI_SHA256, BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN, BRIDGE_BOT_NAME, BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL, BRIDGE_ENV_ID, and Jira credentials directly; this epic replaces those prompts with auto-resolution and guided defaults. This was the root cause of the planning gap identified in dso-gchz: the v2-v3 migration assumed users would manually configure ACLI distribution details that should have been installer-managed. The result is that Jira bridge adoption requires expertise that most users don't have, and misconfigured values cause silent CI failures.

## Success Criteria
1. When a user opts into Jira integration during /dso:project-setup, the installer auto-resolves ACLI version and SHA256 by downloading the latest ACLI binary from acli.atlassian.com, running acli --version to extract the version string, then constructing the versioned tarball URL and computing the checksum locally (TOFU model). ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256 are set as GitHub repository variables via gh variable set — the user is never prompted for these values. TOFU is used because Atlassian does not publish checksums at the distribution endpoint; verification against Atlassian's published release manifest is out of scope for this epic and may be addressed in a follow-on.
2. The installer auto-detects existing Jira environment variables (JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_API_TOKEN) and offers them as defaults. For values not found in the environment, the installer displays a one-line description of the value and the relevant URL (e.g., https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens) before prompting. The user confirms before any secret is copied to GitHub.
3. The installer presents the authenticated GitHub user (from gh api user) and asks whether to use that identity or configure a separate bot account for bridge commits. If the user chooses their own identity, BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN and BRIDGE_BOT_NAME are derived from the API response. For BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL, the installer first checks the email field from gh api user; if null (common when users have private email settings), it attempts gh api user/emails to retrieve the primary verified email; if that also fails (insufficient OAuth scope), it prompts the user. If they choose a bot, the installer guides them through all three values.
4. BRIDGE_ENV_ID is auto-generated deterministically from the GitHub org and repo name (e.g., github-<org>-<repo>) without prompting the user.
5. The installer verifies gh auth status before attempting any GitHub variable/secret operations. If gh is not authenticated, it falls back to printing the exact gh variable set and gh secret set commands to stdout so the user can run them manually. If gh is not installed, it prints equivalent GitHub.com UI navigation steps instead.
6. All Jira bridge configuration values used by the CI workflows (ACLI_VERSION, ACLI_SHA256, JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_API_TOKEN, BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN, BRIDGE_BOT_NAME, BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL, BRIDGE_ENV_ID, jira.project) are fully configured by project-setup when the user opts into Jira — no manual post-setup steps remain for bridge functionality.
7. A team member onboards a net-new project to the Jira bridge using only project-setup, without consulting bridge documentation or setting any variable manually, and the first Jira sync CI run passes. The session is compared against the previous manual setup flow to confirm reduced step count and eliminated expertise requirements.

## Dependencies
cda8-38b5 (Jira bridge integration tests) is a parallel workstream. Integration tests for the auto-resolve installer flow (ACLI download, gh variable set, fallback path) should be added to cda8-38b5's scope if it ships after this epic.

## Approach
Enhance the existing project-setup Step 6.5 Jira bridge sub-wizard to auto-resolve all values that don't require user knowledge (ACLI version/hash, env ID, bot identity from GitHub auth), auto-detect environment variables for values that do (Jira credentials), and guide the user through the remainder with contextual help. Use gh CLI as the primary mechanism for setting GitHub variables/secrets, with manual instructions as fallback. A spike task should validate the ACLI /latest/ -> version extraction -> versioned tarball URL reconstruction -> SHA256 computation round-trip before implementation begins, as the /latest/ endpoint serves a direct binary rather than a tarball.

Supersedes: dso-gchz (bug), dso-7nos (epic)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Adversarial review: 4 findings, 3 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-eeae805657bd1274/adversarial-review-fbd9-ba78.json

comments.body: Epic complete: all 7 stories closed + 1 bug fixed, validation passing, 4 batches committed
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
